message:
test(fcp-conformance): cross-crate invariants for ratelimit TokenBucket + sandbox WASI isolation

Two new integration tests under crates/fcp-conformance/tests/ that
pin downstream-visible contracts for fcp-ratelimit and fcp-sandbox.
Both crates had good per-crate unit coverage but no cross-crate
conformance fence — a refactor inside the implementing crate could
change public behaviour without failing the owning crate's tests, so
the guarantees were implicitly single-owner.

ratelimit_token_bucket_conformance.rs (84 lines, 4 tests):

- token_bucket_multi_permit_acquire_is_atomic: try_acquire_n with
  count > remaining returns false AND leaves remaining unchanged
  (fails the refactor where a partial acquisition drained some
  tokens before the rejection). Exercised with capacity 2.
- refill phase preserved across partial intervals: a partial
  sleep() through the refill window advances the token count
  proportionally, not cliff-style at the interval boundary.
- zero-permit requests are a no-op: try_acquire_n(0) returns true
  without consuming tokens or advancing the refill clock.
- reset() restores full burst capacity: after draining, reset()
  brings remaining() back to the configured capacity.

All four tests are driven under #[fcp_async_core::runtime::test],
matching the rest of the async conformance suite.

sandbox_isolation_conformance.rs (343 lines):

- preopened directories enforce read-only vs writable permissions
  at the WASI Preview2 layer — a mount declared read-only cannot
  satisfy types::open_at(create|write) even under a permissive
  NetworkConstraints.
- path traversal cannot escape a writable mount — canonical `../`
  + symlink escape attempts land on PermissionDenied / NotFound
  without touching the surrounding filesystem.
- strict and mediated profiles fail closed for raw socket
  hostcalls — instance_network + ip_name_lookup + SocketAddrUse
  resolution all return an error variant rather than a populated
  resource.
- deterministic hostcalls are reset per Store/invocation — a
  second Store with the same WasiHostState sees fresh random +
  clock seeds, so sandbox reuse across invocations cannot leak
  state between tenants.

Cargo.toml additions under [dev-dependencies] block (already a
[[test]] runner crate; no feature flags involved):
- fcp-manifest, fcp-ratelimit, fcp-sandbox — path deps on
  sibling workspace crates that the new tests reference directly.
- wasmtime, wasmtime-wasi — workspace deps for the Preview2
  bindings (preopens, types, random, sockets, ip_name_lookup,
  SocketAddrUse, instance_network) used by sandbox_isolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
